dataList.dlg:
- Your battles never fail to bring fair tidings. I shall watch with great anticipation.
- Let's see... Why don't you show me a measure of your strength?

- It is said, "fellow travelers in the same boat will aid one another." That is to say, Aeng-du will. Not I.
- You will, too, Mentor. Lady Moses may have turned a blind eye to your idleness, but if you persist, I shall have no choice but to report your conduct to our two other seniors.
Blades broken, arrows spent... Don't you think it's about time to consider your next gambit?

- It's rather difficult to offer advice here. We have never once stilled our blades, even when cornered—for that is how a Salsu must carry himself.
- Yet you are no Salsus, of course—retreating would not be a stain on your honor. Surely you have tenets of your own to live by.
Like leaves before the autumn gale. Valiantly though they may strive, leaves cannot forever defy the turn of the season. Their fall is but the proper course of nature.
... I know you've got a taste for battles that quickens the blood with thrill... Hmm, even so, you ought not to sacrifice a stone so easily if you wish to win the board.

- Their lives have come to an end. With this, you have bought yourselves a moment's respite.
- Yes, that was certainly not a round for lesser hands to play~ Let's see if this has left only the grandmasters on the board.

- ......
- Looking upon the perished shall not bring our fellows back.
- ... Aeng-du, your words leave such grievous wounds in me.
- Yes, I am not unaware of it. And yet... you must suffer and bear them still—and let these words pain you more, Mentor.

- Your stones' pathway of survival has been sealed. It may be best to resign this round of the game and return another day. - There is a way out... But it's poor form to call out gambits on other people's games. - Pray refrain from commenting if you have no intention to help, Mentor.

- Aeng-du, I wonder why you have suddenly gone quiet.
- There is no advice worth adding, Mentor. They already hold the favorable positions. We could exchange rhymes for poetry, if boredom troubles you so.
- ... Never you mind. I'll just be quiet, too.

- The tilted board is now leaning to equilibrium, if only slightly. This is the opportune moment to press the offense.
- Hmm. Still, a single misplaced stone now could throw the rest into a dead formation. Let's sit back and watch how they'll proceed, shall we?

- Your stones are hanging by a thread. In times such as this, you must consider your gambit with pruden—
- Aeng-du, overzealous prudence curdles into poison. This is not the moment for prolonged deliberation, but a swift decision.
The foes' stones are placed to seize your points by force! This is a moment to stand your ground and meet them head-on instead of falling back.

- Oho!
- Mentor?!
- A round of game that shall go down in history! Didn't expect them to show such a brilliant gambit on the board.
A straight, faithful blow. Truly, there was not a speck of excess legwork in that offense.
Hah! A swordplay most calamitous, contrary, and catastrophic! Well, I suppose there are times when you've got to break away from the orthodox form.
That's it! A sound and proper opening—well-placed stones indeed!
One moment. Might I consult the "Manual for New Employees" awhile? I had not foreseen a situation quite like this...
It would be best to exercise caution here. A sudden change in a foe's bearing seldom comes without purpose—Lady Moses often said as much.

- The foes... It seems you have breached a layer of their morale.
- Have they taken to drinking over the board? Their stones now have fallen into disarray.
Such are the failings of a mind addled by regret.
The pressure upon your party is palpable... I shall seek avenues of escape—pray, watch yourselves in the meantime.
You have shattered its limb. An assault as spirited as that of Lady Ezra's.

- An advantage, yet hardly permanent—do remain vigilant. Mentor, have you any wisdom to impart? - Wisdom to impart? Hm. Here's an art: The rampart shattered apart, if they're smart, they'll dart; their heart astart, with a whizz, their dome to depart. - ... There it is again.

- No, watch out—!
- Becalm yourself, Mentor. They have their ways of returning from the great beyond, besides... They are not our slain fellows.
- .......

- ... This is why I detest working, Aeng-du. Our "work" naturally carries a risk, and to work is to expose ourselves to that very danger.
- A forager who steers clear of the mountains in fear of tigers is bound to starve. Mentor... You know this.

- There once was a time when Mentor possessed the same decisiveness to cut down what must be felled.
- That past has no bearing on the present. Besides, my past is nothing to boast of—I wasn't even the Apex Blade of the Homeland.

- Hmm~ Perhaps with a new blade like yours, even I— - The finest blade on the market for you, even if I must grovel for an advance and take out a loan for it. Anything else, Mentor? - ... I'm never certain if you jest, Aeng-du. My sword is fine...

- Aha. *nom* The placement of their stones is daring. A thrilling watch indeed.
- Mentor... Where did you get those snacks?
- Mm? *nom* These?
- The crumbs are a risk to this expensive equipment.

- I knew victory would—Mentor? You can't clock out yet. To where do you hie?
- This board needs no counting of stones. I'll retreat to my chamber and observe the goings-on of the world.
- ... You're leaving to watch more TV, are you not?

- ... I really must take my own advice, but here's one. Like in a game of Baduk, do not despair over a single bad move; instead, study the board and identify the best path forward from there. Isn't that true, Aeng-du?
- ... Yes, Mentor.

- Aeng-du... What's all this?
- It is work, as is plain to see. Enough days you have spent eating, idling, and amusing yourself with the TV. The time is nigh to remind you of "work"—
- Hadn't I explained that I was merely engrossed in educating myself on the political climate around S Corp.— *sigh* Never you mind. Fine, what is this "work," then?
- First, let us have you seated on a chair, not the floor. How do you intend on reaching the microphone from down there?
